title: signatures.rs has zero tests — sign/verify and known-vector coverage absent
description: The signatures module (sign_request, verify_signature, parse_signature_header) is the most security-critical code in the codebase and has no unit tests at all. There is no test for: (1) sign+verify round-trip, (2) known test vectors against an external oracle (e.g. a pre-computed signature from OpenSSL or Mastodon), (3) parse_signature_header edge cases (missing headers= field, commas inside quoted values, empty signature), (4) verify_signature rejecting a tampered body or wrong key. Without tests, regressions in the signing path will silently break federation — all outbound deliveries will be rejected by remote servers with no obvious error. Fix: add unit tests with at least a sign+verify round-trip using a fixed test keypair, and at least one known-vector test using a signature computed externally (e.g. via openssl dgst -sha256 -sign).
close_reason: All pass 2 fixes applied: circuit breaker reset, persona-scoped 410, SSRF private IP block, tokio::fs, URL scheme validation, Content-Type, page clamp, watch dedup
